sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׂרֹף.-

(1) prop. to suck in, to absorb, to drink in (fchlürfen), to swallow down, i.q. Ch. שְׂרַף, and Syr. ܣܪܦ, see Middeldorpf, Curæ Hexapl. in Job., p. 15; compare under the root שָׂפָה. Hence-

(2) to absorb with fire, i.e. to burn, to consume, as towns, houses, altars, Lev 4:21 8:17 9:11 often with the addition of בָּאֵשׁ Jos 11:9, 11 Jos 11:11 Jud. 18:27 2Ki 23:11 25:9 . Used of the burning of a corpse at the funeral, Jer 34:5 also to burn, and to bake (bricks) by burning, Gen 11:3.

Niphal, Lev 4:12, and-

Pual, pass. of No. 2, Lev 10:16.

Derivatives, שְׂרֵפָה, מִשְׂרָפָה and perhaps שָׂרָף.

II.

שרף

i.q. Arab. سرف and سَرُوَ to be noble. to be born of a noble race; whence شريف a noble, a prince. Hence שָׂרָף No. II.
